tldr: Sketch is a Mac-native vector design tool; Creatie is a browser-based AI-native product design platform. Migration means moving design files, components, and tokens off the .sketch format into Creatie, accepting that some plugin-driven workflows won't carry over.
why_switch: Cross-platform browser-based access for distributed teams
Built-in AI generation for UI screens and icons
Real-time multiplayer collaboration without bolt-on sync
Combined design, prototyping, and developer handoff
what_to_export: All .sketch source files organized by project
Design tokens (colors, typography, spacing) and any plugin exports
Developer handoff specs currently consumed downstream
Image and asset libraries embedded in files
export_method: Open each .sketch file in Sketch and export what travels well: PNG/SVG asset exports for any rasterized or vector content, and SVG exports for symbols where possible. For design tokens, run your existing token plugin (or the built-in JSON export) to produce a tokens file you can re-import elsewhere. For text and layer styles, document them as a style sheet (name, value) since they will need to be recreated as Creatie styles. Save a copy of every .sketch file in cloud storage as a long-term archive even after migration — Sketch files can also be opened in some other tools as a fallback. Many design tools accept .sketch as an import format directly; if Creatie does not, exporting screens to PDF or SVG and rebuilding the component library natively in Creatie may be the most reliable path. Verify what import paths Creatie currently supports before committing to an approach.
import_method: Create a Creatie workspace and project structure that mirrors your Sketch project hierarchy. Import support for .sketch files varies — check Creatie's current docs for the supported path before mass-importing. If direct import is supported, upload files in batches and audit each for fidelity (typography, auto-layout, symbols). If not, recreate the foundation natively: paste in design tokens, rebuild text and layer styles, then rebuild key components from screenshots or SVG exports. Use Creatie's AI generation for new screens going forward and migrate the design system gradually rather than all at once. Set up developer handoff once components are stable so engineers can switch their inspection target. Keep Sketch licensed for at least one cycle as a fallback while migration is in flight.

concept_mapping.note: Import path depends on Creatie's current support

gotchas: Plugin ecosystem does not carry over — anything driven by a Sketch JS plugin will need a Creatie equivalent or replacement workflow
Symbol overrides and nested component logic may not survive automated import; expect cleanup
Prototype links and interactions almost always need to be rewired manually
Typography and font availability differ between local Mac and a browser tool — confirm fonts before committing
Developer handoff URLs will change, so update any docs, tickets, or design system references that point at Sketch Cloud
Keep Sketch licensed during the transition to handle edge cases and legacy files

email.subject: Migrating design system from Sketch to Creatie
email.body:
Hi team,

We are starting a 14-day pilot to migrate our design files and component library from Sketch to Creatie. The motivation is browser-based access for our distributed team, real-time collaboration, and Creatie's AI-native workflow for new screens.

During the pilot we will: inventory Sketch files, export design tokens and SVG assets, rebuild a small but representative slice of the design system in Creatie, and run a developer handoff dry-run with engineering. Sketch will stay licensed for the full transition window so nothing breaks for in-flight work.

If you maintain a Sketch plugin workflow or rely on a specific symbol library, please flag it early — plugins do not carry over and we will need to plan replacements.

Thanks,
Your customer
